articles.title: I Built My Dev Portfolio Using OpenCode and Free-Tier Models. Here's What That Actually Felt Like.
articles.description: The first thing I did after installing OpenCode wasn't build my portfolio. I built a 3D Tic Tac Toe game in React and deployed it. Not because I needed it вЂ” just because I wanted to see what a terminal-based AI coding tool would actually do. Typed a prompt, watched it scaffold something real, deploy
articles.content: The first thing I did after installing OpenCode wasn't build my portfolio. I built a 3D Tic Tac Toe game in React and deployed it. Not because I needed it вЂ” just because I wanted to see what a terminal-based AI coding tool would actually do. Typed a prompt, watched it scaffold something real, deployed it in the same session. That small detour is what convinced me to use it for something I actually cared about. Why I Needed a Portfolio Right Then Just moved back home after living in a hostel. Fre
articles.author: Mohanragul

For me, that project was The Tiebreaker App. Originally, it was a simple tool designed to help users break ties between competing
articles.content: This is a submission for the GitHub Finish-Up-A-Thon Challenge What I Built Every developer has at least one project that starts with excitement but gets left unfinished. For me, that project was The Tiebreaker App. Originally, it was a simple tool designed to help users break ties between competing choices. While the concept worked, it lacked the depth, intelligence, and user experience needed to become truly useful. Instead of letting the project remain dormant, I decided to revisit it with a
articles.author: Muhammad Yasin Khan

articles.title: Anthropic proposes a global slowdown of AI development
articles.description: Anthropic warns that AIs will soon be able to build their own successor, and it's suggesting that companies may have to slow down development.

articles.title: Ahead of its IPO, AnthropicвЂ™s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AIвЂ™s returns
articles.description: Anthropic has been growing at a breakneck pace. The company announced that annualized revenue crossed $47 billion in May, up dramatically from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025. That trajectory faces a real test, though.

articles.title: Are AI chatbots making us lose control of our brains?
articles.description: This week IвЂ™ve been at SXSW London. ThereвЂ™s been music, film, and a lotвЂ”and I mean a lotвЂ”of talk about AI. I also had the opportunity to sit down with Gloria Mark, a psychologist at the University of California, Irvine, who has spent the last 30 years studying how people interact with digital techno
articles.content: This week IвЂ™ve been at SXSW London . ThereвЂ™s been music, film, and a lotвЂ”and I mean a lot вЂ”of talk about AI. I also had the opportunity to sit down with Gloria Mark, a psychologist at the University of California, Irvine, who has spent the last 30 years studying how people interact with digital technologies. Early in her career, the biggest concerns were the potential impacts of internet and email use on our brains. We may laugh those concerns off today, but itвЂ™s true that as the technologies be
articles.author: Jessica Hamzelou

articles.description: Ryan welcomes back Tanya Janca, now part of the OWASP Top 10 team, to discuss what changed in the latest OWASP Top 10 release, how the list shifted from вЂњoutdated componentsвЂќ to a broader software supply chain focus, and why they added memory safety and vibe-coding as awareness items.вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌ
articles.content: Ryan welcomes back Tanya Janca, now part of the OWASP Top 10 team, to discuss what changed in the latest OWASP Top 10 release, how the list shifted from вЂњoutdated componentsвЂќ to a broader software supply chain focus, and why they added memory safety and vibe-coding as awareness items.вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЌвЂЊвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂ‹ вЂЌвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂ‹вЂЌ вЂЌвЂЊ вЂ‹ вЂЊ вЂЊвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹вЂЊвЂЊвЂЌвЂ‹ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЌ вЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ вЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЊвЂЊ вЂ‹вЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌвЂЊвЂЌ вЂ‹вЂЊвЂЌ вЂЊ

articles.title: The Meta hack shows thereвЂ™s more to AI security than Mythos
articles.description: On June 5, 404 Media reported that attackers had been using MetaвЂ™s AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts. Their approach was simple: They asked the agent to link the accounts to email addresses that they controlled, and the agent complied. One attacker broke into the dormant Obama Wh
articles.content: On June 5, 404 Media reported that attackers had been using MetaвЂ™s AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts. Their approach was simple: They asked the agent to link the accounts to email addresses that they controlled, and the agent complied. One attacker broke into the dormant Obama White House account and made pro-Iran posts; others took over accounts with valuable, single-word handles, possibly in order to sell them. AI cybersecurity concerns are nothing new. Since Anthropic anno

articles.title: AT&T and Verizon lose Supreme Court case over fines for selling location data
articles.description: FCC did not violate carriers' right to jury trial, court says in 8-1 ruling.
articles.content: AT&T and Verizon lost an attempt to overturn fines for selling usersвЂ™ real-time location data without consent, as the Supreme Court ruled today that the Federal Communications Commission process for issuing financial penalties did not violate the right to a jury trial. AT&T convinced the US Court of Appeals for the 5th Circuit to overturn its fine last year, while Verizon lost in the 2nd Circuit. The Supreme Court took up the case to resolve the circuit split and reversed the 5th Circuit decisio

articles.content: Valve now says that the delayed Steam Machine PC and Steam Frame VR headset are set to launch sometime this summer. In a Thursday blog post detailing its Verified programs for both pieces of hardware, Valve concludes by saying that "We're excited for players to try your titles on the new Steam hardware once they launch this summer." When the company originally announced the Machine and Frame alongside its new Steam Controller late last year, it said that it would start shipping the new gadgets i

articles.content: This is today’s edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How courts are coping with a flood of AI-generated lawsuits Most days in her chambers, Judge Maritza Braswell, a federal magistrate judge in Colorado, sifts through stacks of documents written by people without a lawyer. The number of these filings has more than doubled compared to before 2023. She puts that jump down to AI. But while AI appears t

articles.description: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey, can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn't look like a computer at all. Tan, who has been featured in The Cut and Wired, went viral earlier this year with a mermaid-themed cyberdeck she made inside an old purse.
articles.content: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey , can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn't look like a computer at all . Tan, who has been featured in The Cut and Wired , went viral earlier this year with a mermaid-themed cyberdeck she made inside an old purse. She has since upgraded it and built an MP3 player and a solar-powered cyberdeck . Tan and countless other DIYers are attracting millions of views showing off the personal computers they've built i
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: Dashlane explains how attackers managed to download encrypted password vaults
articles.description: By targeting large numbers of users, attackers increased their chances of success.
articles.content: Dashlane said that attackers mounted a coordinated hacking campaign against a large base of its users in an attempt to recover as many encrypted password vaults as possible. The password manager provider said fewer than 20 personal user vaults were downloaded before it shut down the operation. In a campaign that started Sunday, the unknown threat actor abused the mechanism that allows Dashlane users to add new devices, such as computers or phones, to their accounts. By abusing Dashlane's program
articles.author: Dan Goodin

articles.description: Back in 2002, Porsche fans sputtered with rage as the Cayenne made its debut at the Paris Motor. More than 20 years later, Porsche now sells more SUVs than anything else in its lineup. Last year, the Macan and Cayenne accounted for 62 percent of all Porsche sales. Now, these SUVs are trolling tradit
articles.content: Back in 2002, Porsche fans sputtered with rage as the Cayenne made its debut at the Paris Motor. More than 20 years later, Porsche now sells more SUVs than anything else in its lineup. Last year, the Macan and Cayenne accounted for 62 percent of all Porsche sales. Now, these SUVs are trolling traditionalists in a whole new way: They've gone electric, with a Cayenne Electric joining a smaller plug-in Macan . And the Cayenne Coupe Turbo is fast enough to have 911 owners looking over their shoulder
articles.author: Lawrence Ulrich

articles.title: GitHub Universe is back: All together now, in the agentic era
articles.description: GitHub Universe is back: returning to the historic Fort Mason Center in San Francisco on October 28вЂ“29, 2026. The post GitHub Universe is back: All together now, in the agentic era appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: If you’ve been following all the AI agent conversations and wondering what’s useful versus what’s just noise, you’re not alone. There are ideas everywhere. What’s challenging is finding the time and a practical path from cool demos to workflows that make your day easier. GitHub Universe bridges that gap. Universe is our flagship event for developers and the teams who support them—builders, maintainers, security practitioners, technical leaders, and partners&md
articles.author: Natalie Guevara

articles.title: Kevin OвЂ™Leary agrees to downsize massive Utah data center
articles.description: Kevin O'Leary agreed to halve the size of his planned 40,000-acre data center in Utah amid mounting pressure from residents and activists, as reported earlier by local affiliate ABC4. The Shark Tank star sent a letter to Utah Senate President J. Stuart Adams on Thursday, saying that he will remove 1
articles.content: Kevin O'Leary agreed to halve the size of his planned 40,000-acre data center in Utah amid mounting pressure from residents and activists, as reported earlier by local affiliate ABC4 . The Shark Tank star sent a letter to Utah Senate President J. Stuart Adams on Thursday, saying that he will remove 19,430 acres from the project, located in and around the Locomotive Springs Waterfowl Management Area. The change comes just days after Adams called on O'Leary to slash the size of his Project Stratos
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.title: The skepticвЂ™s guide to humanoid robots going viral on the Internet
articles.description: Robot demonstrations can distort public perceptions of robotic capabilities.
articles.content: It may appear that humanoid robots capable of handling any task have almost arrivedвЂ”especially when tech companies showcase them performing acrobatic feats or handling household chores. But there is still a significant gap between these robot demonstrations and proving that the same robots can reliably and repeatedly manage such tasks in the real world. The latest wave of robot videos can be particularly tricky, given the human tendency to anthropomorphize objects with a humanoid figure. A robot
articles.author: Jeremy Hsu

articles.title: These LLMs are the best at resisting Russian propaganda
articles.description: Estonian government benchmark shows how dozens of models combat Russia's "strategic narratives."
articles.content: As more people rely on large language models to provide pat answers to complex questions, state governments are understandably worried about those LLMs spouting what they see as dangerous propaganda promoted by foreign adversaries. To help combat this problem, the government-sponsored Estonian Language Institute (ELI) has released a new "Propaganda Resistance" benchmark ranking dozens of LLMs on their ability to avoid "tak[ing] positions on topics that the Russian Federation uses in its strategi
articles.author: Kyle Orland
